forecast-period.text: A weak high pressure system moves into the eastern states overnight. A significant low pressure system moves over waters to the south of SA on Monday and Tuesday reaching western Bass Strait Tuesday afternoon. A another low pressure moves over waters to the south on Wednesday. A ridge of high pressure develops over western and northern parts as a series of troughs and fronts move over the south later in the week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Gusty showers, possible thunderstorms broadly south of a line from Coober Pedy to Marree, tending to patchy rain at times about the agricultural area. Maximum temperatures generally above average. Freshening northerly winds, shifting gusty north to northwesterly ahead of a fresh to strong and gusty westerly change in the west, reaching central districts at night. Damaging winds possible.

Showers about the western districts. Light showers clearing from central and southeastern districts. West to southwesterly winds, fresh at times about the central and southeastern coasts, tending fresh and gusty north to northeasterly across western districts.

Gusty showers. possible thunderstorms and patchy rain about central and eastern districts. Isolated showers about the south of the pastoral districts and West Coast, though scattered about coastal parts with possible thunderstorms. Maximum temperatures generally above average. A fresh to strong and gusty westerly change extending throughout, damaging winds possible. Winds easing from the west later.

Showers about the West Coast and agricultural area, possibly reaching the south of the pastoral districts. Possible thunderstorms near western coasts and Kangaroo Island. Maximum temperatures near average. Fresh and gusty westerly winds.

text.p: Showers about the West Coast and agricultural area, possibly reaching the south of the pastoral districts. Maximum temperatures below average in the south grading to slightly above average in the north. West to southwesterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Thursday: Less than 5 mm about the Pastoral districts south of about Coober Pedy to Marree, increasing to 2 to 10 mm about the northern agricultural area and Flinders district, 10 to 25 mm about the southern agricultural area and increasing to 25 to 40 mm about the Mount Lofty Ranges, Kangaroo Island and the South East, with isolated falls in these areas of 40 to 60 mm.
